original_text: Apple Inc. operates a diversified hardware and services business model, designing, manufacturing, and marketing smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company's product portfolio includes iPhone, Mac, iPad, and wearables such as AirPods, Apple Watch, and HomePod, complemented by services including AppleCare, cloud services, App Store, and subscription offerings like Apple Music, Apple TV+, Apple Arcade, and Apple Fitness+. Revenue distribution spans consumers, small and mid-sized businesses, and education, enterprise, and government markets, with products distributed through retail and online stores, direct sales force, and third-party carriers and resellers. In Q3 2025, Apple reported revenue of $94.036B with a gross margin of 46.49% and net margin of 24.92%, though revenue declined 1.39% YoY and net income fell 5.43% YoY.
revised_text: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company's product portfolio includes iPhone, Mac, iPad, and wearables, home, and accessories comprising AirPods, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats products, and HomePod, complemented by services including AppleCare support, cloud services, and the App Store. In Q3, Apple reported revenue of $94.036B with a gross margin of 46.49% and net margin of 24.92%, though revenue declined 1.39% YoY and net income fell 5.43% YoY.
changes_summary: Removed unsupported claims regarding customer segment revenue distribution and distribution channels per checker issues. Aligned product and service descriptions to strictly match available evidence in C002, removing specific subscription names not explicitly listed. Removed '2025' from the period description to align with the specific period labeling in the calculation references. Retained financial metrics supported by calculation results.
